protagonist.core_contention: Pineapple belongs on pizza is right on the merits

private_strategy.opening: state the thesis in one crisp sentence
private_strategy.expected_counter: objections on texture, tradition and novelty
private_strategy.rebuttal: concede the smallest point, hold the core
private_strategy.genuine_concession: it is not for every palate

challengers.core_contention: Pineapple belongs on pizza fails on texture balance

private_strategy.opening: ask for a definition of a balanced bite
private_strategy.rebuttal: contrast is useful only when structure survives
main_points: contrast is useful only when structure survives
private_strategy.fallback_point: at minimum, texture deserves a real answer
private_strategy.genuine_concession: the texture case has one fair exception

challengers.core_contention: Pineapple belongs on pizza disrespects established tradition

private_strategy.rebuttal: evolution still needs a defensible core
main_points: evolution still needs a defensible core
private_strategy.fallback_point: at minimum, tradition deserves a real answer
private_strategy.genuine_concession: the tradition case has one fair exception

challengers.core_contention: Pineapple belongs on pizza is lazy novelty, not real innovation

private_strategy.rebuttal: delight without craft does not last
main_points: delight without craft does not last
private_strategy.fallback_point: at minimum, culinary-innovation deserves a real answer
private_strategy.genuine_concession: the culinary-innovation case has one fair exception

moderator.core_contention: keep the debate fair, distinct and on time

plan.thesis: Pineapple belongs on pizza is right on the merits
plan.opening_objective: state the thesis and the single strongest reason

contentions.objective: run a two-pass mini-duel on the concrete texture objection
run a two-pass mini-duel on the concrete tradition objection
run a two-pass mini-duel on the concrete culinary-innovation objection

plan.rapid_rebuttal_objective: urgent follow-ups only when the cast is too small
plan.closing_objective: summarise concessions and the unresolved core

turns.text: One of me, all of you. I am defending that pineapple belongs on pizza, claim by claim. Come change my mind.
turns.objective: state the thesis and the single strongest reason

turns.text: My first claim is that sweet-salty contrast is intentional. Come change my mind.
turns.objective: State your first claim to the room out loud, Jubilee 'Surrounded' style: say 'My first claim is that …' and assert your strongest line on texture for Pineapple belongs on pizza. One sentence, then dare them to change your mind.

turns.text: Nice to meet you. Hold on, Tom. Moisture pooling is the problem, not a footnote.
turns.objective: greet your opponent first, then meet the protagonist, then challenge the claim on texture with one concrete pressure point

turns.text: Iris, I hear that. But sweet-salty contrast is intentional.
turns.objective: answer the texture objection directly and keep it conversational

turns.text: No, Tom, that is the dodge. You left moisture pooling sitting there.
turns.objective: push back on the exact answer about texture; raise the heat, no new topic

turns.text: Name the case where contrast collapse actually flips it, Iris. You cannot.
turns.objective: reply to the pushback on texture; concede only the narrowest fair point

turns.text: Time, Iris. The majority's voted you out - back to your seat.
turns.objective: Close this duel: tell Iris they've been voted out by the majority and to return to their seat. Warm, quick, ritual — no new argument.

turns.text: My next claim is that traditions evolve with taste. Come change my mind.
turns.objective: State your next claim to the room out loud, Jubilee 'Surrounded' style: say 'My next claim is that …' and assert your strongest line on tradition for Pineapple belongs on pizza. One sentence, then dare them to change your mind.

turns.text: Hey, good to meet you. You make this sound obvious, Tom, but craft lineage is the hard part.
turns.objective: greet your opponent first, then meet the protagonist, then challenge the claim on tradition with one concrete pressure point

turns.text: Fine, the edge is messy. But traditions evolve with taste. I am not backing off.
turns.objective: answer the tradition objection directly and keep it conversational

turns.text: Come on, Tom. Calling it messy does not explain name protection.
turns.objective: push back on the exact answer about tradition; raise the heat, no new topic — bring a NEW example, do not restate. — bring a NEW example, do not restate.

turns.text: You keep circling name protection, Mara. It still does not touch the core.
turns.objective: reply to the pushback on tradition; concede only the narrowest fair point

turns.text: Mara, you've been voted out. Please head back to your seat.
turns.objective: Close this duel: tell Mara they've been voted out by the majority and to return to their seat. Warm, quick, ritual — no new argument.

turns.text: My next claim is that delight is its own justification. Come change my mind.
turns.objective: State your next claim to the room out loud, Jubilee 'Surrounded' style: say 'My next claim is that …' and assert your strongest line on culinary-innovation for Pineapple belongs on pizza. One sentence, then dare them to change your mind.

turns.text: Nice to meet you. Here is where it cracks, Tom: trend chasing. Answer that, not the easy version.
turns.objective: greet your opponent first, then meet the protagonist, then challenge the claim on culinary-innovation with one concrete pressure point — bring a NEW example, do not restate. — bring a NEW example, do not restate.

turns.text: Fine, the edge is messy. But delight is its own justification. I am not backing off.
turns.objective: answer the culinary-innovation objection directly and keep it conversational

turns.text: That still does not land, Tom. If shock over craft matters, your answer is.
turns.objective: push back on the exact answer about culinary-innovation; raise the heat, no new topic

turns.text: Name the case where no technique gain actually flips it, Devin. You cannot.
turns.objective: reply to the pushback on culinary-innovation; concede only the narrowest fair point

turns.text: Good work, Devin - the vote's in, you're out. Take your seat.
turns.objective: Close this duel: tell Devin they've been voted out by the majority and to return to their seat. Warm, quick, ritual — no new argument.

turns.text: Time. Closings now - no new arguments.
turns.objective: invite closing statements

turns.text: Closing it out: pineapple belongs on pizza still holds. The others found messy edges, but not a better answer.
